22.reason: [The loop breaks when exponent is 0, but the term with exponent 0 (constant term) is still valid and should be included. This causes the last term (with exp=0) to be counted, but the logic assumes input ends at exp=0, which is incorrect because a polynomial may have multiple terms and the last term just happens to have exp=0. Also, if the polynomial has no constant term, this will read past intended input.]

29.reason: [Loop condition should be i < cnt1, not i <= cnt1, because cnt1 is the number of terms (index of last valid term is cnt1-1). Using <= leads to reading uninitialized/invalid data from in1[cnt1].]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition should be i < cnt1, not i <= cnt1, because cnt1 is the number of terms (index of last valid term is cnt1-1). Using <= leads to reading uninitialized/invalid data from in1[cnt1].]

30.reason: [Same issue: should be j < cnt2, not j <= cnt2. This accesses in2[cnt2], which was never properly initialized (loop broke at j = cnt2, so in2[cnt2] is either uninitialized or contains leftover data).]

38.reason: [Accesses out[k+1] when k == cnt3-1, leading to out-of-bounds read. Also, the condition skips merging terms when the next term's exponent is 0, which is incorrect鈥攖erms with same exponent should always be merged regardless of exponent value.]
